themes: AI adoption phase shift: revenue over research

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: 580 replies to 'what are you building with Codex' is a real-time use-case census — high-signal product research for anyone thinking about agent-native tooling.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick's claim that AI agents can now independently reconstruct complex papers from methods+data alone — without code or the paper — has direct implications for agentic product capability floors.

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: Brockman framing GPT-5.5 as 'raising the ceiling of ambition' is the official positioning thesis worth tracking against competitor narratives.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Sharp Figma/Sketch distribution analogy applied to OpenAI vs. Anthropic — the 'artifact as URL' insight is directly relevant to AI product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Brynjolfsson's 25M payroll record study showing 13% drop in entry-level AI-exposed jobs cuts against the LinkedIn job-posting narrative — the data split matters for sales/workforce positioning.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: 50 agents doing 1,500 hours of senior engineer work in 24 hours with rate limits as the only bottleneck is a concrete agentic labor displacement data point.

worthClicking.author: @DataChaz
worthClicking.why: DeepSeek V4 open-source 1.6T parameter model framed against OpenAI's $200/month pricing — highest-engagement framing of the cost disruption story in this batch.

worthClicking.author: @minchoi
worthClicking.why: A concrete solo-creator AI media stack (GPT-Image-2 + Seedance + Suno) producing 100% AI output at 467 likes is a real distribution signal for indie creators.

worthClicking.author: @DataChaz
worthClicking.why: AI-generated App Store screenshots in seconds via @Anything is a practical distribution tool directly relevant to indie dev GTM.

voices.why: Consistent on AI labor data, agent architecture, and PM strategy — the Brynjolfsson payroll analysis and Figma distribution analogy are both sharp and relevant.
The paper-reconstruction finding is a serious capability signal, and his AI policy thread is grounded even if outside the operator's core interests.
Direct OpenAI product narrative from inside — the Codex reply thread and GPT-5.5 positioning posts are primary sources worth tracking.
Surfacing practical indie dev tools (App Store screenshots, DeepSeek V4 demos) with decent reach and relevance to distribution interests.

examples: RoundtableSpace all-caps hype posts (Polymarket bots, engagement bait 'who's online?', AI OPSEC fear content)
aakashgupta newsletter self-promo spam (5+ identical 'subscribe' posts with near-zero engagement)
JaynitMakwana GlobalGPT affiliate thread (coordinated product spam, sub-100 views per post)

emerging.theme: AI adoption phase shift: revenue over research

fading.theme: agent-native PM role & system design bar (continuation)
